competitors.label: Angular Component Lifecycle
competitors.selectionReason: This is the canonical lifecycle reference and the strongest official competitor for a lifecycle-hooks answer.
theirStrengths: It gives the full hook ordering, lifecycle table, and official caveats around state changes and view/content checks.
theirGaps: It is reference-shaped rather than centered on the interview decision of which hook to choose and why.

realWorldUseCases: Scenario matrix interviewers actually expect next
The docs already connect lifecycle hooks to rendering, change detection, queries, and destruction, so the real-world comparison stays close.
actionableExamples: Angular.dev provides more lifecycle-surface area and more hook-level examples than our compact trivia page.
followUpConfusion: It answers many common follow-ups such as execution order, ngOnChanges timing, and destroy behavior, so the broader confusion coverage remains competitive.

realWorldUseCases: Add one scenario matrix that maps common tasks like data fetch, projection, DOM reads, and cleanup to a hook choice.
actionableExamples: Add a second worked example showing projected content versus view queries so the practical hook choice becomes more explicit.
followUpConfusion: Keep one compact note on when afterEveryRender-style APIs differ from the classic class hooks.

competitors.winReason: The official lifecycle guide is broader, but our page now pairs a task-to-hook matrix with projected-content versus view-query examples, which makes the interview decision flow more actionable while broader follow-up coverage stays close.

competitors.label: GreatFrontEnd Angular Basic Interview Questions
competitors.selectionReason: GreatFrontEnd includes a directly relevant lifecycle-hooks section, making it the best interview-prep baseline for this fundamentals topic.
competitors.winReason: Our page is more explicit about hook selection, content-versus-view distinction, and cleanup patterns than the broader interview overview.
theirStrengths: It quickly lists the common hooks and keeps the interview summary concise.
theirGaps: It is lighter on choosing the right hook for a concrete task and on content-versus-view distinctions.

realWorldUseCases: The practical skill is picking the right hook
followUpConfusion: know ngOnChanges (inputs), ngOnInit (init once), ngAfterViewInit (DOM/ViewChild), and ngOnDestroy (cleanup), plus the content-vs-view distinction and the common pitfalls.

realWorldUseCases: GreatFrontEnd names the common hooks, but it does not go as far into the task-to-hook decision framing our page uses.
actionableExamples: Its quick examples are clear enough that the example comparison stays close.
followUpConfusion: The page is lighter on the content-versus-view distinction and on cleanup guidance beyond naming ngOnDestroy.

actionableExamples: Add one small before-and-after example for ngAfterContentInit versus ngAfterViewInit.

competitors.label: Angular University ExpressionChanged Debugging Guide
competitors.selectionReason: This tutorial is lifecycle-adjacent and useful for calibration because it focuses on a classic ngAfterViewInit/change-detection pitfall that often appears as a follow-up.
competitors.winReason: That tutorial is strong on one lifecycle pitfall, but our page is broader on choosing hooks correctly across init, view, content, and cleanup.
theirStrengths: It turns a common lifecycle/change-detection failure into a practical debugging walkthrough.
theirGaps: It is narrower than a full lifecycle-hooks answer and does not cover the main hook-selection map end to end.

realWorldUseCases: The practical skill is picking the right hook
followUpConfusion: know ngOnChanges (inputs), ngOnInit (init once), ngAfterViewInit (DOM/ViewChild), and ngOnDestroy (cleanup), plus the content-vs-view distinction and the common pitfalls.

realWorldUseCases: Angular University grounds the topic in a real ExpressionChanged debugging case, but it stays focused on one pitfall rather than the full lifecycle choice map.
actionableExamples: Its debugging walkthrough is concrete enough that the example comparison remains close.
followUpConfusion: It helps on the expression-changed follow-up, but our page answers more of the likely next questions about content hooks, view hooks, and cleanup.

actionableExamples: Add one teardown example that pairs setup in ngOnInit with cleanup in ngOnDestroy.
